info.title: AI Canvas E-Ink Display API
info.description: HTTP API for controlling a 7.5" B/W e-ink display (800x480 pixels). Send JSON drawing commands to render text, shapes, and images. Coordinate system: (0,0) is top-left, x is horizontal (0-799), y is vertical (0-479).

get.description: Returns display dimensions, supported operations, available fonts, and network info. IMPORTANT: The response includes an 'instructions' field -- read it before sending any drawing commands. You MUST use validate:true on every POST /canvas and check the validation response for text_overlaps and off_screen issues.

fonts.description: Available fonts with metrics for layout planning

yAdvance.description: Vertical advance in pixels. This is the key metric for layout: text block height = N_lines * floor(yAdvance * lineHeight)

post.description:
Execute an array of drawing commands on the display. Commands are processed in order. The display refreshes after all commands complete (~2-3 seconds for full refresh). Returns 429 if a refresh is already in progress; retry after 3 seconds.

RENDERING MODEL:
Coordinate system: (0,0) is top-left. x horizontal (0-799), y vertical (0-479). Commands render sequentially — later commands overwrite earlier ones (no transparency). Each pixel is either black or white. Without a 'clear' command, new commands draw on top of whatever is already on the display. Include 'clear' as the first command to start fresh.

CHARACTER SET:
Fonts support ASCII 0x20-0x7E only (space through ~). Do NOT use unicode, degree symbols, em dashes, curly quotes, or emoji — they will render as missing glyphs. Use alternatives: 'F' not '°F', '--' not '—', plain quotes not smart quotes.

COLORS:
'black' or 'white'. Default is black for drawing ops, white for clear.

OPERATIONS REFERENCE:

- clear: {color} - Clear entire display. Default color: white.
- pixel: {x, y, color} - Single pixel.
- line: {x0, y0, x1, y1, color, thickness} - Line between two points. thickness default: 1. Also accepts x/y as aliases for x0/y0.
- rect: {x, y, w, h, color, fill, radius, stroke, strokeWidth} - Rectangle. fill defaults to TRUE (solid filled). Set fill:false for outline only. radius (rounded corners) only works when fill:true — outlines are always square. stroke/strokeWidth draw a border on top of the fill in a different color.
- circle: {x, y, r, color, fill} - Circle centered at (x,y). fill default: true.
- ellipse: {x, y, rx, ry, color, fill} - Ellipse centered at (x,y). fill default: true.
- arc: {x, y, r, startAngle, endAngle, color} - Arc centered at (x,y). Angles in degrees: 0=right(east), increases clockwise. Draws a 1px outline only, no fill or thickness.
- polygon: {points:[{x,y},...], color, fill} - Polygon, min 3 points, max 64. fill default: true. Outline is always drawn even when filled.
- polyline: {points:[{x,y},...], color} - Connected line segments, min 2 points, max 64. No thickness support.
- text: See TEXT LAYOUT section below.
- image: {x, y, w, h, data, dither} - PNG image. data: base64-encoded PNG. w/h: target display size (image is scaled). dither: 'threshold'|'ordered'|'floyd_steinberg', default 'ordered'. Uses ~48KB RAM during decode.
- gradient: {x, y, w, h, direction, from, to} - Dithered gradient. direction: 'horizontal'(default)|'vertical'. from default: black, to default: white.
- flood_fill: {x, y, color} - Flood fill from seed point. WARNING: internal queue limited to 4096 pixels — large area fills will be incomplete. Use rect for large solid areas instead.
- clip: {x, y, w, h} - Set clipping rectangle. All subsequent drawing is confined to this rect. Only one clip active at a time (new clip replaces old).
- unclip: {} - Remove clipping rectangle.
- raw_bitmap: {x, y, w, h, data} - 1-bit bitmap. data: base64-encoded packed bits. MSB is leftmost pixel, rows padded to byte boundaries. Bit value: 1=white, 0=black.
- qr: {x, y, size, data, color, ec} - QR code. x,y=top-left corner. size=square dimension in pixels (modules scale to fit). data=string to encode (URL, text, etc). ec: error correction 'L'|'M'(default)|'Q'|'H'. Includes 2-module quiet zone.

METADATA:
Any command can include an optional 'meta' object with freeform key-value pairs. Metadata is stored and returned by GET /canvas, enabling multi-agent coordination. Example: {"op":"text","x":30,"y":72,"text":"72F","meta":{"type":"weather","source":"weather-agent"}}. Frame-level metadata can be set in the request object: {"commands":[...],"meta":{"agent":"my-agent"}}.

TEXT MEASUREMENT:
Use POST /measure to get pixel-perfect text dimensions before rendering. Supports batch mode (array of requests). Returns {width, height, lines, truncated, yAdvance, lineHeight}.

TEXT LAYOUT — CRITICAL FOR CORRECT POSITIONING:

Fonts: 'sans' (9/12/18/24pt, regular+bold), 'mono' (9pt bold only). Default when omitted: sans 12pt regular. Nearest available font is used if exact match not found.

Font metrics (use these to calculate vertical spacing):
  sans  9pt: yAdvance=22px, default line height=26px
  sans 12pt: yAdvance=29px, default line height=34px
  sans 18pt: yAdvance=42px, default line height=50px
  sans 24pt: yAdvance=56px, default line height=67px
  mono  9pt: yAdvance=18px, default line height=21px

The y parameter is the TOP of the text block. However, the first line's baseline is placed at y + yAdvance. This means visible glyph pixels start at approximately y + 0.6 * yAdvance (for capitals). The TOTAL HEIGHT of a text block with N lines is: N * floor(yAdvance * lineHeight).

Text supports newline characters (\n) for manual line breaks within a single text command.

Spacing rules to PREVENT OVERLAPS:
1. Next element below text: next_y >= text_y + N_lines * floor(yAdvance * lineHeight)
2. Text inside a filled rect: rect height must be >= yAdvance. Set text y = rect_y for bottom-aligned text. For visually centered text, set text y = rect_y - (yAdvance - rect_h) / 3. Negative y values are safe — pixels above the screen are clipped. Example: 60px bar at y=0 with size 24 (yAdvance=56): set text y=-10 to center visually.
3. Align shapes (circles, rects as bullets) vertically with adjacent text: set shape_center_y = text_y + 0.8 * yAdvance. Example: text at y=100 size 12 (yAdvance=29) — place a bullet circle at cy=123.
4. Align different-sized text on the same baseline: set y values so y_a + yAdvance_a == y_b + yAdvance_b.
5. Estimate line count for wrapped text: chars_per_line ~ maxWidth / (yAdvance * 0.45) for sans.

Alignment: Without maxWidth, x is the anchor point (left edge for 'left', center point for 'center', right edge for 'right'). With maxWidth, x is always the LEFT edge of the text box and text aligns within that box.

valign ('top'|'middle'|'bottom'): Only effective when maxHeight is also set. Without maxHeight, valign has no effect.

overflow:
- 'clip' (default): Lines beyond maxHeight are hidden.
- 'truncate': Last visible line gets '...' appended.
- 'shrink': Automatically tries smaller fonts (24->18->12->9 sans regular) to fit. Note: bold is lost when shrinking.

COMMON MISTAKES (read this before rendering):\n1. ALWAYS compute text block height before placing the next element: block_h = num_lines * floor(yAdvance * lineHeight). Next element y >= prev_y + block_h.\n2. ALWAYS estimate wrapped line count when using maxWidth: num_lines = ceil(char_count / floor(maxWidth / (yAdvance * 0.45))). Account for manual \\n adding extra lines.\n3. NEVER set maxHeight smaller than the text needs — it silently clips lines. Required: maxHeight >= num_lines * floor(yAdvance * lineHeight).\n4. NEVER eyeball y positions — calculate every y from the previous element's y + block_h.\n5. Keep status bar text short — long strings get clipped at display edge (800px).\n\nBATCHED RENDERING:
For complex scenes (many commands or polyline/polygon points), split into multiple smaller requests with refresh:'none', then send a final request with refresh:'full'. Each batch draws to the framebuffer without refreshing. The final batch triggers one display refresh. Example: batch 1 draws header (refresh:'none'), batch 2 draws chart (refresh:'none'), batch 3 draws footer (refresh:'full'). Always include 'clear' only in the FIRST batch.

PRACTICAL LIMITS:
- Max JSON payload: ~32KB (not the 2MB HTTP limit — the JSON parser buffer is 32KB).
- Max polygon/polyline points: 64.
- Flood fill queue: 4096 pixels.
- E-ink refresh: ~2-3s. Display returns 429 if busy.
- E-ink retains image when powered off — no need to redraw on boot.

zone.description: Target zone ID for partial update

refresh.description: Display refresh mode. 'full': full EPD refresh after render (default). 'partial': partial refresh. 'none': draw to framebuffer only, no display refresh. Use 'none' for batched rendering — send multiple small requests with refresh:'none', then a final request with refresh:'full' to flush everything to the screen in one refresh. This lets you build complex scenes that exceed the single-request JSON memory limit (~10KB effective for point-heavy payloads).

validate.description: Enable render validation with per-command bounding boxes, text overlap detection, and off-screen warnings

dryRun.description: Dry-run mode: validate layout WITHOUT drawing to the display. Returns the same validation data as validate:true but does NOT render pixels, commit to the framebuffer, or trigger a display refresh. Use to iterate on layout before rendering. Implies validate:true. Response status will be 'dry_run'. Circuit breaker: after 3 consecutive dry-runs the 4th auto-promotes to a real render (dry_run_overridden:true). Response includes dry_runs_remaining.

hello.summary: Hello World - size 24 text centered on display

text_in_box.summary: Text inside a filled rect (rect h >= yAdvance)

stacked_text.summary: Correctly spaced stacked text (each y += yAdvance)

dashboard.summary: Dashboard layout with proper spacing using font metrics

value.text:
9:00 AM  Team standup
10:30 AM  Design review
1:00 PM  Lunch with Alex
3:00 PM  Sprint planning
"The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now."

validation.description: Present when validate:true was requested. IMPORTANT: Always check text_overlaps and off_screen after each render -- if non-empty, fix your layout and re-render.

commands.description: Per-command bounding boxes in render order

bbox.description: [x, y, w, h] bounding box of this command's output

off_screen.description: True if any part extends beyond 800x480

lines.description: Number of text lines rendered (text commands only)

truncated.description: True if text was truncated by maxHeight

text_overlaps.description: Pairs of command indices [i, j] where two text bounding boxes overlap. Non-empty means layout bug -- fix spacing and re-render.

off_screen.description: Command indices with content extending beyond the 800x480 display. Non-empty means content is clipped -- adjust positions and re-render.

get.description: Returns the command log for the currently displayed frame.
Returns current display contents as a PBM image.

post.description: Clear the entire display to white or black.
Create named zones for partial display updates.

get.description: Returns uptime, free memory, WiFi RSSI, and display status.

post.description: Returns pixel-perfect dimensions for text without rendering. Supports single object or array for batch measurement. Use this to plan layouts precisely instead of estimating.

maxWidth.description: Max width for word wrap (pixels). 0 = no wrap.

oneOf.description: Batch mode: array of measurement requests

200.description: Measurement result (object for single, array for batch)

width.description: Actual pixel width of rendered text

height.description: Total pixel height of text block

truncated.description: Whether text would be truncated at maxHeight

DrawCommand.description: A drawing command. Each operation uses different parameters - see the POST /canvas description for per-operation details.

y.description: Y coordinate (vertical, 0=top edge, 479=bottom edge). For text: top of text block. First baseline renders at y + yAdvance.

radius.description: Corner radius (rounded rect) or alias for r

fill.description: Fill shape (default: TRUE — shapes are solid by default). Set false for outline only. For rect: rounded corners (radius) only work when fill is true.

text.description: Text content. ASCII 0x20-0x7E only. Supports \n for line breaks.

valign.description: Vertical text alignment. REQUIRES maxHeight to be set — has no effect without it.

maxWidth.description: Max text width in pixels for word wrap. Also changes alignment behavior: with maxWidth, x is always the left edge of the text box and text aligns within that box width.

maxHeight.description: Max text height in pixels. Lines exceeding this are clipped, truncated, or shrunk per overflow setting.

overflow.description: Text overflow when maxHeight exceeded. 'clip': hide overflow (default). 'truncate': last line gets '...'. 'shrink': try smaller fonts (loses bold).

points.description: Array of {x,y} points. Polygon: min 3, max 64. Polyline: min 2, max 64.

startAngle.description: Arc start angle in degrees. 0=right(east), increases clockwise.

endAngle.description: Arc end angle in degrees. 0=right(east), increases clockwise.

data.description: Base64-encoded data (PNG for image, packed bits for raw_bitmap)

ec.description: QR error correction level. L=7%, M=15%(default), Q=25%, H=30%.

meta.description: Optional freeform metadata. Stored and returned in GET /canvas for multi-agent coordination. Example: {"type":"weather","source":"my-agent","updated":"2026-03-06T07:00Z"}
